summary: DHH reports on Denmark's experience two weeks after dropping all COVID restrictions, arguing it validates the decision. Despite high infection rates and 30% test positivity, Danish society has moved on, with health authorities viewing Omicron as a blessing that builds natural immunity. He contrasts Denmark's approach favorably against countries like the US that maintain restrictions, particularly around children in schools. He acknowledges uncertainties about future variants but argues Denmark is responding to current science rather than fear.
key_points: Denmark dropped all COVID restrictions and society functioned normally despite high Omicron infection rates, with ICU admissions and deaths-from-COVID remaining stable

Danish health authorities reconsidered vaccinating young children and acknowledged that vaccinated and unvaccinated contract Omicron equally, reducing the vaccine argument to individual protection against serious illness
The distinction between hospitalized-with-COVID versus hospitalized-because-of-COVID is critical and was being misread by international commentators
Other countries ignored Denmark's data-rich example, much like they ignored Italy's early outbreak data at the pandemic's start
US restrictions on children, including school masking and outdoor distanced eating even after vaccination, are contrasted unfavorably with Denmark's lighter approach
money_quotes: Omicron is basically heralded as a blessing. The ticket out of the pandemic.

Now that it's clear that vaccinated and unvaccinated people contract the virus in equal proportion, the pro-vaccine argument is reduced to the fact that it offers good protection for adult individuals against serious illness.

The facts that are emerging from that intense level of inquiry into the virus show clearly that omicron's spread is not mitigated by vaccinations, that the spread is unstoppable outside of extreme, draconian lockdowns.
On a broad, societal level we no longer need to fear this variant to a degree that it dictates how we live.
They've chosen to react to The Science of today, and have stopped fearing omicron as a disease that requires society-wide restrictions, and get on with life.
At the fear of hubris, it seems the future of the pandemic is already here. It's just not evenly distributed.
key_insight: Denmark's data-driven decision to drop all COVID restrictions amid Omicron proved successful, exposing other countries' continued restrictions as driven more by fear than by science.
